user_message: Can you help me map the stakeholders? The customer (VanArsdel Ltd) is asking about migration architecture.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-13 14:17 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-21 13:51 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with migration architecture. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-24 17:52 EST
Subject: migration architecture - Action Required

There's been a change in our timeline for migration architecture. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - VanArsdel Ltd
Date: 2024-01-16
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on migration architecture from our last session.

[00:05:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:07:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:10:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:15:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:19:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:24:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:26:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:29:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:33:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
